data.title: Borregaard ASA: Proxies for the Annual General Meeting 16 April 2026
data.description: Ahead of Borregaard's Annual General Meeting, Mr. Helge Aasen, Chair of the Board of Directors in Borregaard ASA, has been granted voting rights for 7,906,608 shares in the company through proxies. These proxies are specific to the Annual General Meeting scheduled for April 16, 2026. As a result, Mr. Helge Aasen now holds voting power over 7.9% of the total outstanding shares in Borregaard ASA.
Annual General Meeting scheduled in cBrain A/S.
Bekaert provides an update on its share buyback program and liquidity agreement.
Company buys back 10,000 of its own shares as part of its buyback program for $100,000, aiming to increase shareholder value.

data.title: Global study shows energy industry ramping up investment in autonomous operations by 2030 as AI reshapes performance
data.description: A recent study conducted by Schneider Electric surveyed 400 senior executives from the energy and chemicals sectors in 12 different countries. The research focused on the impact of artificial intelligence on performance and its role in enabling autonomous operations.

data.title: Bitcoin Setting Up For A Sell Signal, Says Legendary Trader, But Where Exactly Is The Bottom?
data.description: Renowned trader and chart analyst, Peter Brandt, shared concerns on Thursday about a pattern indicating diminishing bullish strength for Bitcoin. With nearly half a century of experience, Brandt noted a rising wedge formation, a bearish technical signal suggesting a potential shift from an uptrend. This pattern is characterized by the convergence of price within two upward-sloping trendlines, showcasing higher highs and higher lows that point to waning bullish momentum. Brandt hinted at a possible upcoming downturn for Bitcoin, with the cryptocurrency at risk of a sell signal as per the rising wedge formation. Another notable cryptocurrency commentator, Michaël van de Poppe, also highlighted similar patterns. Brandt's analysis on Bitcoin's future prospects comes amid growing concerns in the market. (Note: 864 Characters)
